conclusion: Google Maps emerged from three 2004 pre-IPO acquisitions — Where 2 Technologies (tile/AJAX web maps, Sydney, undisclosed <$50M), Keyhole (~$35M → Google Earth, John Hanke → Niantic), ZipDash (~$2M real-time traffic) — orchestrated after Sequoia tipped Larry Page ("We like the web"). Bret Taylor PM'd launch; satellite imagery and Maps API (2006) made maps a platform for Zillow, Uber, DoorDash. Apple Maps (2012) failed without Google's data moat; Google regained iOS in 3 months. Ben and David grade an A (not A+ vs Instagram): fundamental internet infrastructure, API monetization ($7/1K requests), local search front door — but heavy CapEx (Street View, Ground Truth, Skybox satellites) and years of losses before capture.
background: 2003: PM Bret Taylor's "Search by Location" had zero users. Yahoo Maps push and Sequoia intro to Where 2 — Lars and Jens Rasmussen's dynamic tile map in Noel Gordon's Sydney bedroom. Larry Page meeting: AJAX rewrite satisfied "we like the web." Parallel buys: Keyhole (Earth Viewer, Hanke), ZipDash (4 Nextel phones traffic). Feb 2005 Maps beta Slashdotted; satellite view drove "find my house" viral adoption. 2006: largest online mapping provider; Maps API unleashed mashup era (PadMapper, Trulia). Dependency on Tele Atlas/NavTeq ended via Street View cars, Ground Truth (Megan Quinn), VuTool — booting vendors Oct 2009. Mobile: iPhone 2007 shipped Maps with Google data; Android 2009 added turn-by-turn ( withheld from iOS during platform war). Apple Maps 2012 disaster → Google native iOS app 10M installs in 2 days. Waze acquired 2013 ($1.3B).
important_facts: Three 2004 acquisitions: Where 2 (undisclosed, estimated <$50M), Keyhole ~$35M, ZipDash ~$2M — ZipDash became dominant mobile Maps despite seeming insignificant.
Google Maps API launched mid-2006; enabled mashups (PadMapper, Zillow, Trulia) — foundational to Uber/DoorDash-class businesses per episode.
October 2009: Google replaced Tele Atlas/NavTeq with proprietary map data from Street View / Ground Truth programs.
December 2012: Google Maps iOS app reached 10M+ installs within ~2 days of Apple booting Google from default Maps on iOS 6.
Current API pricing cited ~$7 per 1,000 requests; ~5M customer API keys — local ads and API dual revenue vs years of running Maps deeply unprofitable.

mental_model.name: Maps as Platform, Not Feature
mental_model.components: Lars insight: big interactive map is the UI platform, not printable directions. AJAX tiles + Web 2.0 made 100× UX jump. Google bundled acquisitions under Taylor/Hanke geo org, then invested billions in owned data (cars, satellites) to escape vendor tax. Maps becomes second search box (local intent) plus B2B API layer capturing ~10% of value created (Shopify 2.5% analogy). Moat = data + latency + distribution (Android, default iOS era then win-back app). Competitors need capital and time Google had as profitable search subsidized bet.
mental_model.application: Platform acquisitions should be evaluated on ecosystem enablement, not standalone P&L early. Owning core data beats licensing when suppliers can choke strategic products. In mobile OS wars, controlling maps + navigation is strategic weapon (Apple learned painfully).

competitive_advantage: Google Maps stacks proprietary global basemap, real-time traffic (ZipDash/Waze), Street View imagery, routing scale, and developer API standardization. Engineering depth (vector tiles post-static era) and Search integration for local ads. Weaknesses: multi-billion CapEx; regulatory scrutiny on bundling; Apple continues improving Maps; Mapbox well-funded but uphill. Self-driving tailwind slower than expected when episode recorded. Versus Instagram A+: Maps is high fixed-cost infrastructure with lower margin profile than pure software social — but wider enabling impact across economy. Skybox Imaging (2014) satellites; Ground Truth derived map geometry from Street View stills. Lars → Google Wave (Slack precursor) → Facebook Workplace → weav.io running app. Foursquare 10.0 rating carve-out request for Google PMs. Waze $1.3B vs ~$10–20M Where 2 — acquisition price irrelevant versus platform value. Self-driving tailwind slower than 2012 hype when episode recorded.

key_insights.view: Sequoia passed Where 2 but won anyway via Google intro.
key_insights.question: Could Where 2 have stayed independent?
key_insights.answer: Pre-2004 capital markets couldn't fund global map data buildout; Sequoia skeptical of map business models. Flickr/YouTube sold early too — Maps needed Google's loss tolerance and search cash flow.

key_insights.view: ZipDash looked trivial, became mobile Maps core.
key_insights.question: Why pay attention to tiny acquisitions?
key_insights.answer: $2M ZipDash (4 Nextel phones) foreshadowed mobile dominance — 90%+ usage on phones today. Integration into backwater mobile team inverted significance vs web launch.

key_insights.view: Apple Maps proved data moat length.
key_insights.question: Could Apple displace Google quickly?
key_insights.answer: iOS 6 (2012) lacked decade of Google Ground Truth, user probes, and routing ML — turn-by-turn sent users wrong places. Google shipped full iOS app in 3 months using same API backend; 10M installs in 2 days.

key_insights.view: Maps API created more value than Google captured early.
key_insights.question: Value creation vs capture?
key_insights.answer: Free then cheap API spawned trillion-dollar adjacent markets (Uber, etc.); Google ran Maps deeply red ~10+ years. Capture rising via ads + $7/1K API — platform captures small slice of enormous enabled GMV.

key_insights.view: Alumni tree spans Salesforce to Niantic.
key_insights.question: Who came from Maps?
key_insights.answer: Bret Taylor → FriendFeed → Facebook CTO → Quip → Salesforce CPO; John Hanke → Niantic/Pokémon Go; Megan Quinn → Square/KPCB; Lars → Wave → Facebook Workplace → weav.io. Geo group as Google talent factory.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Maps local ads and API licensing grow as second search surface; monitor disclosure of maps revenue within Google Services and CapEx for geo data vs Apple competition.
Apple Maps investment continues but Google iOS app retention proves hybrid equilibrium — Apple's privacy narrative vs Google's data quality.

golden_quotes: We like the web. — Larry Page at end of Where 2 pitch; Lars committed to AJAX rewrite on the spot.
It had zero users per day. — Bret Taylor on pre-Maps "Search by Location" feature.
None of this would be possible without Google Maps API. — David linking mashups to Uber, Zillow, DoorDash.

events.event: Bret Taylor's Search by Location at Google — zero usage
Acquires Where 2, Keyhole (~$35M), ZipDash (~$2M); AJAX rewrite
Google Maps beta; Slashdot launch traffic
Satellite/aerial imagery; surpasses MapQuest/Yahoo; Maps API released
Street View program begins; VuTool acquired; iPhone ships with Google Maps
Drops Tele Atlas/NavTeq; own data only; commercial API pricing
Android turn-by-turn navigation; withheld from iOS Google Maps app
Apple Maps replaces Google on iOS 6; widespread errors
Google Maps iOS app; 10M+ installs in 2 days
